reviews.review_description: My favorite rhythm game on the oculus with a diverse soundtrack and visuals. The only Japanese oculus game that feels like it had some heart put into it.

reviews.review_title: Lacks the joy of the original
reviews.review_description:
I should have requested a refund immediately after seeing that the original Latin music was missing, but I thought I might get used to the new soundtrack, and I hoped Sega would do the right thing and release the original soundtrack as DLC.

It looks like Sega has abandoned the game and the chances of getting the original music are slim.

The new soundtrack is mostly unbearable. I can’t get into it at all.

Samba de Amigo VR has lost its soul as Sega has.

Sega used to rank right up there with Nintendo.  They’ve fallen so far.
More music please 80/90/00s top hits

Whilst yes the whole game is perfect, I can’t get over the fact they did the Mockyplayer style of multiplayer, where you cannot see players actually moving, just their figures doing reactions, and it ain’t right for the price your charging, and advertising multiplayer.

With Beat Saber, Synth Riders, and Audio Trip I can play without ever moving my feet, but Samba de Amigo gets me moving, shaking, and jumping all over the place! It leaves me feeling happy and energized! Come join the fun! 😊

Very enjoyable game. I love the poses during the game play!

I am missing the multi verse mode in the menu.

It indicates that it is also a multi player game however, it only has the 20 player battle mode only.

I would like to see 1 v 1 mode.

reviews.review_title: Better than I had ever hoped!
reviews.review_description:
Game is great… already sweating after a few easy songs haha!  The campaign mode is neat, gaining followers and leveling up.  Plenty of songs in the base game, with a couple of cheap song packs on top of that already… keep ‘em coming!  Deluxe Edition is worth it for the extras.  

The problem with a lot of rhythm games is that there’s no point besides a “high score,” but there’s a lot here,  from the campaign where you earn “followers,”, to an online pyramid battle with 20 players where you also level up, and lots of cosmetics to unlock and buy with your earned credits.

Here’s a tip: the game defaults to a pretty strange auto-height setting BUT…

I would turn that auto-height off in settings, and then adjust the rings manually.  I found that moving the Ring Height UP (+30 in my case) and the Ring Depth closer to your body (-20 in my case) really makes for a better experience.  In any case, try it at default for a couple songs, and then do some adjusting to see what fits… for me it was better to move the rings up, and closer to the body to have a greater range of maraca-shaking movement.
complete crap.  terrible music.  no Samba de Janeiro song in the samba game!  are you kidding?  I would like my money back.  not worth it.

I'm new to VR, so I insta-bought this when I saw it, Samba De Amigo with the maracas was a crazy good time back in the day.
The wii version wasn't near as good, I have regretted selling my dreamcast maracas for years.
But the regret has subsided now I can okay this VR version... The controls are so accurate and intuitive, my kids could play and dominate on their first attempt.
Being in the game is just wild, its a sensory overload!

If I have a compliant it's gotta be the music, my kids love the selection but there aren't many from the original, and not a huge amount of Latin songs in general.

If you like rhythm games it's a must buy!

I just gotta say, when I played this on VR for the first time, I was absolutely stunned by the creativity and effort put into the game! It really makes people feel the power of music in their bodies and they can feel the groove at ease! Really is fun and looking forward to more songs being added to the game to continue the love of Samba De Amigo VR! Thank you Sega! You’re awesome!

💎25d.bio.link💎 


Amazing game for all the retro heads!  Is got the classic Sega feel  and  brings you back but in all is glorious VR now! 

Leave me a thumbs up if my review helps you!
I don’t think this game is fun if you don’t have nostalgia attached to it. It’s essentially a baby version of any more fun VR rhythm game. wasn’t a big fan

This game is extremely fun and a good little arm workout. I am a wheelchair so mostly play games in bed and I can play this game easily and it gives my arms a workout which is great as I struggle to do so.
Love everything about this game, makes me so happy and absolutely exhausted at the same time 😅

To be honest, it's really, really fun.
The direction is different from Beat Saber or Synth Rider.
Samba de Amigo is more suitable for arcades and parties.
The songs are fun, and the judgment is not strict. Additionally, random games and poses ensure you won't get bored playing the same song.

Even if you're not interested in music, these are songs you've probably heard at least once somewhere. 

I don't like DLC, but considering the composition of the DLC's songs, I think it's well worth the price. I've already purchased all the DLC.

Highly recommended.

reviews.review_title: Samba de Amigo is now almost perfect 😀
reviews.review_description:
This is my fairly detailed Samba de Amigo virtual party review. I hope it helps with your decision on a purchase or not.

I have been a fan of Samba de Amigo since it was originally released on the good old Dreamcast. I own both games on Dreamcast as well as the Wii version too. While there are control issues on both consoles ( I have 3 sets of Dreamcast maracas) it seems they have finally found the perfect control scheme in VR. I've played a handful of normal and hard songs and so far I'm almost perfecting each song. There is a learning curve if you have only played the original games since they have added a few extra elements like sliding. However it won't take you long to adjust. Sega has also added another new gameplay mechanic in the form of a question mark bubble. This acts as a timed modifier where it could be hitting home runs, slowdown and speed up of notes etc. I'll be honest and say that it could have been omitted. It would be great if there was an option to not have this or the slide mechanics. Unfortunately sega hasn't given that option and it's forced on you.

When it comes to the graphics on the quest 3 it's a dream come true for a Samba de Amigo fan, you truly are teleported in to the colourful and samba infused world. If you're getting this on the quest 3 it does have a mixed reality mode which is just outstanding. I was so amazed I ended up missing shakes as I was taking the world breaking through. It really is an experience to be had. I cannot fault this game when it comes to the graphics.

There are a few different modes to get yourself lost in but I do miss the arcade mode from the previous games where you played through 3 stage's. Here you now only have rhythm party where you can select practice, rhythm play and ranked play. You also have your mixed reality modes where you have 3 difficulty options all with 3 stage's. They are short but still fun. There is also a worldparty type mode where you play through challenges to gain followers for amigo. I would liken this to the challenge mode found in the original games but fleshed out a little more. With each challenge you will be tasked not just to finish the song but to achieve certain conditions like achieving a certain rank or shake X number of rapid shakes etc. You will also unlock in game items in the in-game store via this mode as well.

So unlike the original games there is an in-game shop which as you play the game and rank up you will earn coins you can then use these coins for accessories for amigo and other things like different maracas etc. There are some items you can't buy and will only unlock for you after you reach a certain level. There are a few items that require special coins which you can purchase with the normal coins you earn, although it will take a good amount of game play time if you want to unluck / buy everything in the store.

So why have I only given the series I love only 4 stars in the latest entry? Well while there are 40+ songs not all are suited to the game's overall setting and experience. There is a massive amount of songs missing from the original Dreamcast games and all those songs fitted in perfectly with the experience. It's also strange they never tried to get all the original songs as the Wii version had most of them. It's just crazy not to have Samba de Janeiro or the samba de amigo songs. It also looks like there are no future plans to rectify this. There is a future dlc pack which will include 2 songs from the original games but all other songs pack dlc's are either from various other sega games or more obscure selections. This is why I can only give the game 4 stars. I do hope one day sega manage to get more of the original songs in to the game but that is looking unlikely.

Overall I'm still happy with my purchase and glad I can experience being in the world of my all time favourite rhythm music game franchise. The experience is more than I could have ever dreamed of. It was a long wait but by God it was worth the wait. Thank you sega for providing an experience such as this, I hope you continue working on VR experience if they will be anywhere near the experience of samba de amigo: virtual party.
